title: Fabric elements on a scrapbook page
materials: fabric scraps (cotton or linen, washed and pressed)
bookbinding-board-2mm (backing for a panel if needed)
Mod Podge or equivalent decoupage medium (as alternative adhesive)
sources: SYNTHESISED — fabric-on-paper scrapbooking is a contemporary craft. No pre-1928 PD source.
notes: How to adhere fabric swatches to a scrapbook page without the fabric puckering or the adhesive bleeding through. Three methods: (1) PVA-backed fabric (paint diluted PVA on the back of the fabric, let dry, then the fabric handles more like paper and cuts cleanly), (2) double-sided tape (fastest, clean, good for small pieces), (3) ironing fusible web to the fabric back before cutting (most secure for textured fabrics). Covers: the adhesive choice guide, cutting fabric (no fraying: seal the cut edge with a thin line of PVA on a brush, let dry), combining fabric panels with paper photos, using washi tape as a frame. Note: fabric-backed pages are bulkier — allow extra album spine space.
